spec_match.rationale: The embedded player now runs in a sandboxed opaque-origin iframe, and the parent calls `navigator.mediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId()` without reaching into the child frame.

interactive.rationale: Real getUserMedia, enumerateDevices, setPreferredSinkId, child audio controls, and an iframe-boundary probe make the cross-frame flow observable.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now demonstrates the cross-origin-without-postMessage claim by sandboxing the child frame without `allow-same-origin`.

edge_cases.rationale: The opaque-origin sandbox branch is covered, but the Permissions Policy `speaker-selection` allow-vs-deny branch is still not shown.

references.rationale: Links to chromestatus.com/feature/5098834847072256 and the W3C mediacapture-output spec section.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, warn-block uses --accent-rose, iframe is dashed-bordered with --border-black.

openQuestions.title: Show the permissions-policy gate
openQuestions.detail: setPreferredSinkId is gated by Permissions Policy 'speaker-selection'. Add a second iframe with allow-attribute set vs missing to demonstrate the gate — currently invisible.

summary: Resolved the major cross-origin gap: the player now runs in an opaque-origin sandboxed iframe and the parent no longer uses postMessage after setting the preferred sink. The remaining follow-up is the permissions-policy comparison.
